topic: Case study: designing pipelines from scratch for several scenarios
key_concepts: A full pipeline design connects the abstract principles into concrete architectural choices.
Scenario 1: Retail demand forecasting. Requires structured windowing, temporal splits, rolling aggregations, and strict avoidance of look-ahead leakage.

Scenario 2: Medical image classification with patient metadata. Requires a hybrid multimodal pipeline: image augmentation and a pretrained CNN extractor combined with standardized numerical patient features, fused late in the model.
Each scenario begins by defining the target and the observation unit, mapping out the available data sources, selecting the proper validation strategy, and choosing the appropriate representations.
examples: In the demand forecasting scenario, the unit is (Store, Product, Week). The target is sales volume in Week T+1. The split is strictly chronological. The features include rolling averages from Week T-1 to T-4. In the medical scenario, the unit is an individual patient exam. The features combine continuous blood pressure (robustly scaled) with X-ray embeddings extracted via a frozen CNN.

relations.All Chapters: Acts as the practical synthesis of Part I (foundations), Part II (tabular), Part IV (time-series/image), and Part V (hybrid/pretrained).

figures.caption: Arsitektur dua pipeline kasus studi: deret waktu tabular dan hibrida multimodal.
figures.description: A side-by-side architectural diagram. Left: Retail forecasting showing time windows and temporal splits. Right: Medical hybrid showing image CNN extraction joining with tabular patient data.

exclusions: Do not provide explicit code blocks for the full pipelines; keep the focus on the architectural decisions.
Keep the scenarios high-level to stay within the word budget.
